steps.title: Identify the triangle's sides
steps.content:
The small square marks the right angle. From the figure:
  • Hypotenuse (opposite the right angle) = 21
  • One leg = 6
  • Unknown leg = a

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle: a² + b² = c²
Here a² + 6² = 21²
Isolate a²: a² = 21² − 6²
Take the square root: a = \sqrt{21^{2}-6^{2}}

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: \sqrt{21^{2}-6^{2}} — exactly what the Pythagorean theorem gives. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: 21² − 6² — this is a², not a; missing the square root. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \sqrt{21−6} — subtracts the sides before rooting; no Pythagorean basis. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: 21 − 6 = 15 — simple subtraction of sides, not valid for finding a leg. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
